title: phegopteris ridleyana
intro: Phegopteris ridleyana, also known as the Malaysian leathery fern, is a species of fern belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant has been found to have health benefits including reducing inflammation, fighting against free radicals, and promoting wound healing. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves of Phegopteris ridleyana are often used to make tea or infusions, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
